content:
SUBJECT: AI Gym Day 25: The anti-hallucination brief

The free 14 days got the reps moving. The paid track is where the habit has to become professionally useful. Today belongs to research and evidence: one practical workout, one usable output, one more piece of your AI working system.

TODAY'S WORKOUT
---------------
RESEARCH & DISCOVERY | PUSH | 20 min

THE BRIEF:
Set rules that make it harder for the model to invent certainty.

THE SITUATION:
Use a research task you might normally give to AI. Today you will constrain it before it answers.

PROMPT SCAFFOLD:
Do not copy this blindly. Adapt it to your work.

"I am working on [real situation]. My goal is [specific outcome]. The audience or user is [person/team/customer/reader]. Here is the source material or context I am allowed to share: [paste safe material]. The constraints are [length, tone, deadline, format, privacy, things to avoid]. Help me with: Set rules that make it harder for the model to invent certainty. Before producing the final output, tell me what assumptions you are making and what information would improve the result."

YOUR REPS:
1. Tell the model to separate known facts, source-backed claims, plausible hypotheses, and unknowns.

2. Ask it to include "I do not know" where evidence is missing.

3. Audit the output for any claim that should have a source but does not.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
Your output is good if:
- Unknowns remain visible.
- Source-backed claims are not mixed with guesses.
- You remove or flag at least one overconfident sentence.

TRANSFER:
This transfers to market scans, evidence reviews, competitor research, stakeholder questions, policy checks, and any moment where fluent uncertainty could otherwise sneak into the room wearing a confident tie.

COACHING NOTE:
The professional habit is not "trust but verify". It is "design the task so verification is possible".

BEHAVIOURAL DESIGN:
Create a reusable anti-hallucination instruction. This is your implementation intention for today: after you finish the workout, decide exactly where this output will live and when you will use it again.

MINIMUM VIABLE REP:
If today is chaos, do the smallest honest version: spend five minutes creating the input and five minutes reviewing the output. Do not aim for a masterpiece. Aim for a usable artefact and one clear improvement. Keeping the chain alive matters because skill comes from repeated contact with real work, not from admiring the concept from a safe distance.

---------------
Use only material you are allowed to share with your chosen LLM. Anonymise anything sensitive. Treat AI outputs as drafts, not authority. Verify factual claims before using them in public, financial, medical, legal, HR, or high-stakes contexts.
The AI Habit: https://harrysharman.com/projects/ai-habit/
